Common name EN: flame lily / climbing lily / gloriosa lily
Seller / source: Amsterdam Tulip Museum shop / Amsterdam Bulb Co. (as shown on card)
Flowering period: Late summer if started in spring
Planting depth: Lay long tuber flat with eye 5 cm below surface
Spacing: 20 cm pot; train shoots upward with support
Light: Sun and warmth; at least half-day sun
Soil / drainage: Rich compost with sand; moist but not wet while growing; dry rest after foliage dies back
Hardiness: Tender; frost-free; not outdoor-hardy in Heiligenhaus
Intended location: Pot indoors / greenhouse; not the outdoor tulip bed
Image source: Cropped from Amsterdam bulb card photo supplied by Miguel
Image rights/status: Temporary private/reference crop from packaging; replace with own flower photo after blooming
Notes: Card text: climbing bulbous plant with leaf-tip tendrils; native of Zimbabwe. Culture relatively easy with sun and warmth. Not a tulip. Toxic plant; all parts of Gloriosa contain colchicine-related alkaloids and should be kept away from pets/children.

Notes: Card text: Imperial Crown; brick-red drooping flowers under a whorl of funky leaves; introduced to Holland from Turkey in late sixteenth century; hardy and easy-going; best planted in small groups in a sunny spot; deer and rodent proof.
